visualGoal: Reassure a new Star Citizen pilot that the gamepad they own is enough, and make the smart spend obvious: a Stream Deck for binds, or a HOSAS pair for pure precision — not a reflexive single-stick HOTAS. Low product density by design; lean on the axis table, decision tree, and buy order. Every embedded image must be a verified in-house product photo.

realProductPhotosNeeded.product: Gamepad (Xbox Wireless Controller and/or Sony DualSense)
realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: The article's entire subject is 'is a controller enough' yet there is NO verified gamepad image. Highest-value missing asset. A real Xbox controller and/or DualSense product photo would anchor the piece. Currently the gamepad is text-only.

Headline all-in-one HOTAS in the buying ladder (189 controls, twin throttles, $249.99). Text-only because no verified image. Source a real X56 photo before it can appear as a shotgrid/gearpick or before certification if the ladder should show it.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Realistic dark home-battlestation desk at magenta-and-cyan neon rim light, split composition: an open flat surface on the left sized for a real gamepad product overlay, an open flat surface on the right sized for a real flight-stick product overlay, a faint Star Citizen-style cockpit glow on the monitor behind, no fake branded hardware, no fake UI text, 16:9 editorial cover. Space-bay IgnitionSim look.
editorialCompositePrompts.negativePrompt: fake logos, fabricated gamepad, warped controls, impossible ergonomics, tiny unreadable text, procedural gradient art, invented UI

infographicPrompts.title: What each controller actually reaches
content: Ship needs 6 analog axes: pitch, yaw, roll, forward/back, vertical strafe, lateral strafe
Gamepad: 4 sticks-worth + 2 triggers (~14 buttons) — strafe cramped onto buttons
Single-stick HOTAS: 4-5 analog (stick + throttle), 30+ buttons — still leaves 2 strafe axes on buttons
HOSAS (two sticks): 6-8 analog across two grips — reaches all six
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center chart, large readable text, space-bay magenta/cyan palette, checkmark vs button icons per axis row

infographicPrompts.title: Gamepad, Stream Deck, HOTAS, or HOSAS?
content: New / casual? -> Keep the gamepad, tune the deadzone, spend $0
Flies fine but drowning in binds? -> Add a Stream Deck, not a stick
Also fly DCS / MSFS? -> Buy a HOTAS (start ~$170 T.16000M FCS)
Precise space combat is the point? -> Skip single stick, go HOSAS (two sticks)
infographicPrompts.style: Branching flowchart, four terminal nodes, IgnitionSim space-bay styling, product-photo placeholders at each terminal for Codex overlay

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult space-sim curator Val Chen at a tidy dark battlestation, one hand resting on a gamepad on the desk with a flight stick parked to the side unused, calm honest-broker energy, product overlay space beside the hands, practical not fantasy, 4:5 portrait, magenta/cyan rim light.

screenshotsOrGameMediaNeeded.what: Star Citizen keybindings / flight settings screen showing per-axis deadzone, response curve, and saturation controls
screenshotsOrGameMediaNeeded.why: Supports the 'tune the pad first' tip callout and the cheapest-fix FAQ; a real settings screenshot would let a reader replicate the deadzone fix.
screenshotsOrGameMediaNeeded.notes: Use official CIG media or an in-house capture; do not fabricate a UI.
